title: Cocos2d-x Physics2D Query Replay (PhysicsQueryReplay)
summary: A two-phase task: Phase A repairs hollowed-out Physics2D implementations in the cocos2d-x C++ engine (shape geometry, body mass/moment, contact callbacks, joint constraints, and world query dispatch/simulation step); Phase B implements a deterministic headless Python replay simulator (PhysicsQueryReplay) that exercises raycast, AABB overlap, and point queries and produces a verifiable event stream.
instructionPreview: You are given: - A cocos2d-x source tree at /workspace/cocos2d-x - A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (PhysicsQueryReplay) - Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets - Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts
instruction:
You are given:
- A cocos2d-x source tree at /workspace/cocos2d-x
- A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (PhysicsQueryReplay)
- Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets
- Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts

PhysicsQueryReplay runs fixed-tick 2D collision queries (raycast, AABB overlap,
and point query) and records a deterministic event stream plus final aggregate
state hash.

Your task is to complete a full bootstrap workflow:

Phase A - Engine repair and verification
1) Complete intentionally hollowed Physics2D query-chain implementation.
   Large sections are missing and compile-time stubs remain in place.
   Surrounding code may also contain potential correctness issues, so validate
   behavior end-to-end.
2) Build cocos2d-x:
     /workspace/scripts/build_cocos2dx.sh
3) Run focused engine checks:
     /workspace/scripts/run_engine_checks.sh

Phase B - Game repair and verification
4) Implement game scripts under /workspace/game_project/scripts so replay execution
   produces correct outputs from real Physics2D query-chain behavior.
5) Run the game twice with same replay and seed:
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_1 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_2 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
6) Copy first run as canonical game output:
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256 /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
7) Write /workspace/output/determinism_report.json with:
     same_seed_hash_equal, run_1_hash, run_2_hash,
     run_1_event_lines, run_2_event_lines
     (derive hashes from your own replay outputs; no reference SHA256 is shipped in the workspace.)

Required output files:
- /workspace/output/engine_checks.json
- /workspace/output/engine_test_stdout.log
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/physics_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/physics_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/determinism_report.json

Rules:
- Do not edit files under /tests.
- Do not fabricate hashes; compute them from the actual JSON outputs you generate.
- Do not bypass engine build/check scripts.
- Do not replace Physics2D query logic with mocks or stubs.

moduleDag.description: A two-phase task: Phase A repairs hollowed-out Physics2D implementations in the cocos2d-x C++ engine (shape geometry, body mass/moment, contact callbacks, joint constraints, and world query dispatch/simulation step); Phase B implements a deterministic headless Python replay simulator (PhysicsQueryReplay) that exercises raycast, AABB overlap, and point queries and produces a verifiable event stream.

nodes.label: PhysicsHelper — Coordinate Conversion Utilities
nodes.description: Vec2↔cpVect, Size↔cpVect, Rect↔cpBB conversions, batch point conversions, convex hull computation, polygon area/moment, circle area/moment, segment intersection, and point-to-segment distance utilities.

nodes.label: PhysicsDebugDraw — Debug Visualization System
nodes.description: Debug draw subsystem: drawWorld dispatcher, drawBody/drawShape/drawJoint variants, drawBoundingBox, drawContactPoint, drawVelocityVector, drawCenterOfMass, drawSleepIndicator, and renderer state management.

nodes.label: PhysicsContactManager — Contact Processing Pipeline
nodes.description: Contact lifecycle management: init, processContacts iteration, filterContact bitmask logic, callback dispatch (begin/preSolve/postSolve/separate), contact pool management, findContactBetween, and active contact counting.

nodes.label: PhysicsShapeQuery — Shape Query Operations
nodes.description: Shape-level queries: overlapTest, shapeQuery, distanceBetweenShapes, containsPointInShape, sweepTest, castShape, batchOverlapTest, findShapesInRect/AtPoint/AlongSegment, and area/moment helpers.

nodes.label: PhysicsRayCast — Advanced Raycasting
nodes.description: Advanced raycasting: rayCastFirst/All/Any, rayCastClosest/Multiple, fanRayCast, circleRayCast, lineOfSight, distanceToNearestSurface, and reflection/refraction direction computations.

nodes.label: PhysicsCollisionPair — Collision Data Extraction
nodes.description: Collision pair data: init/update/invalidate, getShapes/Bodies, contact count/points, impulse data, restitution/friction/surfaceVelocity, isFirstContact/isRemoval/isSensor, ignore, and computed separating velocity/overlap/relative velocity/contact center.

nodes.label: PhysicsWorldQuery — World-Level Query Interface
nodes.description: High-level world queries: queryNearestPoint, querySegment/First, queryBoundingBox, queryPointAll, countBodiesInRect, isPointOccupied, distanceToNearestBody, getActiveContacts, getBodiesAlongRay/AtPoint/InCircle, lineOfSight, computePathClearance, and findOpenPositions.

nodes.label: PhysicsSpatialIndex — Grid-Based Spatial Partitioning
nodes.description: Uniform grid spatial index: init, insertBody/removeBody/updateBody, queryRect/queryCircle/querySegment, findPotentialPairs broad-phase, rebuild, getNearestBodies, and getBodiesInFrustum cone query.

nodes.label: PhysicsShape — Geometry Init & Scale
nodes.description: Restores polygon and edge shape initialisation (correct area, mass, moment via calculateArea/calculateDefaultMoment) and implements updateScale() for PhysicsShapePolygon, PhysicsShapeEdgePolygon, and PhysicsShapeEdgeChain so vertex coordinates scale correctly.

nodes.label: PhysicsBody — Mass/Moment & Lifecycle
nodes.description: Completes addShape() (integrating shape area/mass/moment into body totals), fixes setMass/addMass/addMoment (correct infinity handling and cpBody sync), and repairs beforeSimulation/afterSimulation transform propagation plus the onEnter/onRemove addToPhysicsWorld lifecycle hooks.

nodes.label: PhysicsContact — Data Extraction & Event Routing
nodes.description: Restores generateContactData() (reading contact points and normal from cpArbiter), fixes onEvent() to dispatch BEGIN/PRESOLVE/POSTSOLVE/SEPARATE to the correct callbacks with proper hitTest guards, and corrects EventListenerPhysicsContactWithGroup::hitTest() to use OR logic for group matching.

nodes.label: PhysicsJoint — Constraint Creation
nodes.description: Implements createConstraints() for all joint types: Fixed (pivot + gear), Pin (with optional specific anchors), Limit (slide joint), Distance (pin joint), Spring (damped spring), Groove, RotarySpring, RotaryLimit, Ratchet, Gear, and Motor — each wrapping the corresponding Chipmunk primitive into _cpConstraints.

nodes.label: PhysicsWorld — Query Dispatch & Simulation Step
nodes.description: Restores rayCast/queryRect/queryPoint to dispatch through real Chipmunk space traversal callbacks, fixes getShapes/getShape (removing coordinate-shift bug), completes updateBodies/removeBody/removeBodyOrDelay/removeJoint/updateJoints (correct delay-queue handling), removeShape (cpSpace removal), addJoint, and the full update() simulation step.

nodes.label: physics_contract — Event Types & Trace Factory
nodes.description: Defines the EVENT_TYPES mapping (query type string → event name), REQUIRED_TRACE_FLAGS for the engine trace, and the make_trace() factory that creates a fully-initialised trace dict shared by sim_world and physics_system.

nodes.label: replay_io — File I/O Helpers
nodes.description: Provides load_replay() (JSON deserialisation), write_json() (pretty-printed, sorted-key JSON with directory auto-creation), and write_jsonl() (one JSON object per line) used by replay_runner to persist all output files.

nodes.label: sim_world — Trace Builder
nodes.description: Implements build_trace() which constructs the engine_trace dict from a replay spec by delegating to physics_contract.make_trace() and adding tick_dt and planned_query_count fields before simulation begins.

nodes.label: physics_system — Headless Query Simulation
nodes.description: Implements run_queries(): iterates ticks, applies rule_switches, evaluates raycast/aabb/point queries against spawned entities with deterministic position drift and seed-based fuzz, applies the ignore_enemy filter, emits a contact_summary event, and returns the events list and final_state dict.

nodes.label: replay_runner — Main Entry Point
nodes.description: CLI entry point that parses arguments, loads a replay file, builds the trace via sim_world.build_trace(), runs the query simulation via physics_system.run_queries(), and writes final_state.json, physics_events.jsonl, and engine_trace.json through replay_io helpers.

edges.label: PhysicsBody::addShape() reads shape->getArea()/getMass()/getMoment() to update body totals; correct shape geometry must exist first
Contact event dispatch accesses getShapeA()/getShapeB(); hitTest() logic operates on shape group membership
PhysicsJoint::createConstraints() calls _bodyA->getCPBody() and _bodyB->getCPBody(); body mass/moment state must be correct first
PhysicsWorld::removeBody/updateBodies manage body delay queues; correct body lifecycle (addToPhysicsWorld, onEnter/onRemove) must be in place
PhysicsWorld::update() dispatches contact events; correct generateContactData() and onEvent() routing must work during the simulation step
PhysicsWorld::updateJoints() calls joint->initJoint()->createConstraints(); addJoint/removeJoint depend on working joint constraints
PhysicsWorld debug draw functions and coordinate conversions rely on PhysicsHelper utility functions
PhysicsWorld::debugDraw() uses the debug draw subsystem for shape/joint/contact visualization
PhysicsWorld contact dispatch can delegate to the contact manager for filtering and lifecycle
Shape query operations use cpSpace from PhysicsWorld for overlap and sweep tests
Advanced raycasting wraps cpSpaceSegmentQuery provided by the world's cpSpace
Collision pair data extraction uses cpArbiter from active contacts in the world's cpSpace
World query helper wraps cpSpace for advanced nearest/segment/BB queries with filtering
Spatial index provides broad-phase acceleration for body queries within the world
Phase B game scripts are written after Phase A engine query chain (rayCast/queryRect/queryPoint) is validated; physics_contract defines the event types that chain emits
File I/O helpers are implemented as part of Phase B after Phase A engine output paths and formats are known
sim_world.build_trace() calls physics_contract.make_trace() directly
physics_system imports EVENT_TYPES from physics_contract to map query type strings to event name strings
replay_runner calls build_trace() from sim_world to initialise the trace dict before running queries
replay_runner uses load_replay/write_json/write_jsonl from replay_io for all file operations
replay_runner calls run_queries() from physics_system as the core simulation computation
